title: Advanced OSPF
summary: Advanced OSPF covers area design, LSA types, route summarization, and path optimization.
diagram: Area 0 backbone with NSSA and totally stubby areas
example: Use OSPF summarization at ABRs to reduce LSA flooding and table size.
troubleshooting: Review LSA database consistency and ABR placement for suboptimal SPF results.

title: BGP Basics
summary: BGP is a path-vector protocol that exchanges routes between autonomous systems.
diagram: AS65001 <eBGP> AS65002 with iBGP route reflection inside each AS
example: Prefer one ISP by manipulating local preference or AS path prepending.
troubleshooting: Check peering states, update-source settings, and prefix filtering policies.

title: Route Redistribution
summary: Redistribution exchanges routes between different routing protocols with metric translation controls.
diagram: OSPF domain <-> EIGRP domain via boundary router
example: Use route-maps and tags to avoid loops after two-way redistribution.
troubleshooting: Look for route feedback loops and inconsistent metric seed values.

title: MPLS Fundamentals
summary: MPLS forwards packets using labels and enables scalable VPN services.
diagram: CE -> PE -> P -> PE -> CE with label switched paths
example: LDP assigns labels while IGP carries loopback reachability.
troubleshooting: Validate IGP and LDP adjacency first; no labels means no MPLS forwarding.

title: VPN Technologies
summary: VPNs provide private transport over shared infrastructure using encryption or segmentation.
diagram: Branch Router -> Internet -> HQ Router (IPsec tunnel)
example: Site-to-site IPsec protects branch traffic across public WAN links.
troubleshooting: Check Phase 1 policy match, interesting traffic ACL, and NAT traversal settings.

title: QoS
summary: QoS prioritizes delay-sensitive traffic using classification, marking, queuing, and shaping.
diagram: Class-map -> Policy-map -> Service-policy on WAN interface
example: Apply LLQ for voice traffic and shape bulk data to preserve latency.
troubleshooting: Verify DSCP marking at edge and ensure policy is attached in the right direction.

title: High Availability
summary: High availability reduces downtime using redundant links, first-hop redundancy, and fast convergence.
diagram: Dual core with HSRP/VRRP and ECMP uplinks
example: HSRP provides a virtual gateway IP so clients keep a consistent default route.
troubleshooting: Check hello timers, interface tracking, and split-brain conditions.

title: Network Automation Basics
summary: Automation improves consistency through APIs, templates, and version-controlled changes.
diagram: Git repo -> CI pipeline -> API call -> Device config
example: Use RESTCONF to query interface state and validate post-change health.
troubleshooting: Ensure API credentials, certificate trust, and idempotent templates.
